message:
chore: close beads ft-e34d9.10.6.2 and ft-e34d9.10.8.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3460,7 +3460,7 @@
 {"id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.1", "title": "[asupersync][cutover] Runtime dependency eradication (tokio/smol/async-io)", "description": "Remove remaining forbidden runtime dependencies in scoped crates and align manifests/features with target architecture.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Remove direct forbidden runtime deps from scoped manifests or isolate behind explicit non-default compatibility gates.\n2. Validate dependency graph is coherent and minimal after cleanup.\n3. Unit tests validate feature-flag behavior and compatibility boundaries.\n4. Integration tests ensure cleaned dependency graph still supports required workflows.\n5. E2E scripts validate runtime behavior in cleaned build profiles with structured logs.", "notes": "Dependency cleanup is a correctness and maintainability requirement, not just aesthetic debt work. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Include failure-injection and degraded-path scenarios (timeouts/cancellation/error classes) with explicit assertions and diagnostics. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "BronzeHarbor", "created_at": "2026-02-22T22:26:53.365911Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-11T21:31:23.233290Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["asupersync", "cleanup", "dependencies", "e2e-tests", "integration-tests", "logging", "quality-gate", "sequencing", "test-coverage", "test-logging", "user-impact"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-2h1ha", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.4.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.5.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.2", "title": "[asupersync][cutover] Compile-time/runtime guards against forbidden regressions", "description": "Add static and CI guardrails that fail fast when forbidden runtime imports/dependencies are reintroduced.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Add guard scripts/tests to block forbidden runtime imports and manifest regressions.\n2. Guardrails provide actionable failure messages and remediation hints.\n3. Unit tests cover guard classifier behavior and false-positive edge cases.\n4. Integration tests validate guard behavior in CI workflows.\n5. E2E scripts include guard outputs in artifact bundles and structured logs.", "notes": "Preventing backslide is as important as migration itself; codify the rule in automation. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. Include failure-injection and degraded-path scenarios (timeouts/cancellation/error classes) with explicit assertions and diagnostics. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-22T22:26:54.358018Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-08T22:01:34.556881Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["asupersync", "cleanup", "e2e-tests", "guardrails", "integration-tests", "logging", "quality-gate", "sequencing", "test-coverage", "test-logging", "user-impact"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.2", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.2", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1640, "issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.2", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Implemented cutover runtime regression guardrails: added policy docs/asupersync-cutover-runtime-guardrails.json, validator script scripts/validate_asupersync_cutover_runtime_guards.sh (with self-tests), e2e scenario tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards.sh (structured logs + failure injection + recovery + determinism check), wired into scripts/e2e_test.sh scenario registry/dispatch, and updated docs/asupersync-migration-baseline.md with execution commands + artifact locations.

Validation evidence:
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_cutover_runtime_guards.sh --self-test --output docs/asupersync-cutover-runtime-guard-validation.json (pass)
- TMPDIR=/tmp bash tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards.sh (pass; remote offload confirmed)
- bash scripts/e2e_test.sh --list | rg ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards (present)
- TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (fails due pre-existing unrelated test/API breakage in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/orchestration_integration.rs and proptest_replay_fault_injection.rs)
- TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails due pre-existing clippy lint in frankenterm/scripting/src/events.rs:143)
- TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due pre-existing formatting drift in unrelated files)

Primary log bundle:
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards_20260228_072846.jsonl
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards_20260228_072846.stdout.log
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards_20260228_072846.report.ok.json
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards_20260228_072846.report.fail.json
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards_20260228_072846.report.recovery.json
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_e34d9_10_8_2_cutover_runtime_guards_20260228_072846.report.repeat.json", "created_at": "2026-02-28T07:41:54Z"}, {"id": 2018, "issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.2", "author": "PlumDeer", "text": "Reopened on 2026-03-08 after bv --robot-alerts flagged this issue as stale in_progress with 7+ days of inactivity and no recent bead-thread progress. Clearing the stale claim so it can be triaged again by active agents.", "created_at": "2026-03-08T22:01:34Z"}]}
 {"id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.3", "title": "[asupersync][cutover] Canary rollout, rollback rehearsals, and fail-safe drills", "description": "Execute staged canary rollout and rollback rehearsals for runtime-migrated paths under controlled fault conditions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Define canary cohorts, promotion criteria, and rollback triggers for runtime cutover.\n2. Run rollback rehearsals that prove fast, safe recovery from failed rollout states.\n3. Unit tests validate rollout state-machine helpers and policy gates.\n4. Integration tests exercise promotion/rollback transitions in automation.\n5. E2E scripts run canary+rollback drills with exhaustive structured logs and artifacts.\n6. Enforce user-disruption budgets (latency/error/recovery thresholds) during canary promotion decisions.\n7. Heavy rehearsal and soak-like runs are executed via rch exec -- ... with reproducible evidence capture.", "notes": "Rollout safety must be practiced, not assumed. Treat rehearsal evidence and user-impact budgets as release prerequisites. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-22T22:26:55.410847Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-02-22T23:42:29.787759Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["asupersync", "cutover", "e2e-tests", "integration-tests", "logging", "quality-gate", "rollout", "sequencing", "test-coverage", "test-logging", "user-impact"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.6.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.7.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
-{"id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.4", "title": "[asupersync][cutover] Final go/no-go review and closure evidence package", "description": "Assemble final technical decision package proving migration goals met and residual risks accepted/mitigated.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Produce consolidated evidence package covering tests, benchmarks, incidents, rollback rehearsals, and post-cutover soak outcomes.\n2. Include explicit crash/restart persistence evidence proving deterministic recovery and no silent user-state loss for critical workflows.\n3. Document unresolved risks, ownership, and follow-up controls explicitly.\n4. Unit/integration/e2e gate results are included with reproducible command artifacts.\n5. Final review checklist is machine-checkable and human-readable.\n6. Closure only when all prerequisite beads are closed and evidence completeness checks pass.", "notes": "Future maintainers should be able to understand exactly why and how this migration was accepted without external context. Closure evidence must include structured logging outputs, diagnostic traces, and reproducible command artifacts, including rch-executed heavy validation runs and explicit user-impact outcomes for CLI, robot, watch, and session workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. This bead must include cross-module integration tests that validate boundary behavior, data contracts, and degraded/failure interactions under representative workloads. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.", "status": "open",  {"id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.5", "title": "[asupersync][cutover] Post-cutover soak and user-journey confidence gate", "description": "Run prolonged post-cutover soak and user-journey confidence validation to ensure stability under realistic sustained workloads before final closure.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Define soak matrix covering long-running ft watch, robot orchestration loops, session persistence, remote SSH flows, restart cycles, and mixed workload bursts.\n2. Execute sustained soak with failure injection and confirm no critical task leaks, deadlocks, message-loss, or unbounded latency regressions.\n3. Include crash/restart persistence verification scenarios (state durability and deterministic recovery for capture/session/search/control-plane workflows) as mandatory soak gates.\n4. Unit tests validate soak harness controls, anomaly detectors, and pass/fail classifiers.\n5. Integration tests verify automated rollback trigger logic derived from soak metrics and incident signals.\n6. E2E scripts run user-journey matrix with detailed structured logs, anomaly timelines, and reproducible artifact manifests.\n7. Heavy soak/test jobs are executed via rch exec -- <command> and command history is archived for audit.", "notes": "Final cutover confidence requires long-horizon behavior proof, not just short CI passes. This bead captures stability evidence users actually depend on. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.",  {"id": "ft-e34d9.11", "title": "[asupersync][alien] Program: hyperreactive supremacy architecture", "description": "World-dominance performance/reliability program for FrankenTerm: make the system radically more reactive/responsive while preserving hard robustness and operational safety. This program applies alien-artifact principles (formal inference, decision theory, provable guarantees, explainability) plus extreme profile-driven optimization (baseline/profile/prove/implement/verify loops with isomorphism checks) to produce measurable superiority in latency, throughput, stability, and recovery behavior.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Deliver an end-to-end architecture and execution plan that improves user-perceived responsiveness (CLI, robot mode, watch, session/snapshot, remote flows) without feature loss.
2. Every child track must include explicit unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts with deterministic fixtures/seeds and reproducible replay artifacts.
3. Every child track must emit detailed structured diagnostics/logging (timestamp, run_id, scenario_id, correlation_id, operation, latency, queue/backpressure state, error_class, recovery action).
4. All heavy benchmark/soak/verification workloads must run via rch exec -- <command> with auditable command and artifact manifests.
5. Program closure requires evidence-backed superiority vs current baseline on latency/throughput/reliability/recovery metrics, plus explicit rollback safety and residual-risk ownership.", "notes": "Design intent: avoid local micro-optimizations that increase fragility. We are optimizing for globally coherent behavior under real swarm load, adversarial failure, and long-running operation. Mathematical rigor is mandatory: decisions must be explainable (evidence ledger), safety claims must be test-backed, and performance claims must be benchmark-backed. This bead is self-contained by design so future contributors can recover full intent without external docs.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:10:56.797636Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-02-23T01:39:57.544508Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-23T01:39:57.544497Z", "close_reason": "Merged into canonical reactivity execution tree ft-2p9cb (see ft-2p9cb.7 no-loss mapping gate).", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["alien-artifact", "asupersync", "e2e-tests", "extreme-optimization", "integration-tests", "logging", "performance", "program", "quality-gate", "reactivity", "reliability", "sequencing", "test-coverage", "test-logging", "user-impact"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.11", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.11", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:19:43.106372Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1255, "issue_id": "ft-e34d9.11", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Radical plan-space optimization pass applied (2026-02-23) using alien-artifact + extreme-optimization principles.

Why this pass:
- Push beyond local tuning into globally coherent, mathematically constrained responsiveness/reliability architecture.
- Make user-perceived reactivity (not just throughput) an enforceable first-class gate.
- Ensure every bead remains self-contained with explicit unit/integration/e2e + structured logging + reproducible evidence expectations.

What was revised:
1) Tightened all track-root acceptance criteria (ft-e34d9.11.1 .. ft-e34d9.11.10) to explicitly require:
   - unit tests
   - integration tests
   - end-to-end scripts
   - measurable p50/p95/p99 + reliability budgets
   - deterministic replay artifacts
   - rch-based heavy-run evidence

2) Added second-wave high-leverage beads and wired them into critical closure path:
   - ft-e34d9.11.2.5  NUMA-aware shard placement + cache-coherence minimization
   - ft-e34d9.11.3.5  Constrained MPC tail-latency controller
   - ft-e34d9.11.5.5  Perceptual QoE + adaptive frame-budget engine
   - ft-e34d9.11.7.5  Compositional model-checking across scheduler/recovery/dataplane
   - ft-e34d9.11.9.5  Autonomous incident game-day synthesizer + remediation trainer
   - ft-e34d9.11.10.5 Comparative supremacy benchmark treaty + reproducibility publication pack

3) Overlaid dependency chain so these are mandatory for final signoff:
   - 2.5 -> 3.5 -> (5.5, 7.5) -> 9.5 -> 10.5 -> 10.4
   - 10.1/10.2/10.3 were additionally connected where needed to consume new evidence and controls

Optimization intent (future-self guidance):
- Treat hardware locality, tail control, perceptual QoE, compositional proof, and game-day preparedness as one coupled system.
- Prevent false confidence from isolated microbench wins by requiring cross-track evidence convergence before go/no-go.
- Preserve complete feature scope while reducing tail-risk and incident-recovery uncertainty.

Validation snapshot after this pass (ft-e34d9.11*):
- bead count: 57
- missing acceptance criteria: 0
- missing notes: 0
- missing explicit unit/integration/e2e/logging/rch text: 0
- missing quality/test/logging labels: 0
- dependency cycles: 0 (br dep cycles)
- terminal nodes: only ft-e34d9.11.10.4 (expected final closure gate)
- bv --robot-suggest asupersync_11 findings: none

Net result: plan is now deeper, more user-outcome-centric, and better protected against hidden performance/reliability regressions before implementation.", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:26:11Z"}, {"id": 1259, "issue_id": "ft-e34d9.11", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Follow-up optimization: priority signal normalization applied.

- Rationale: avoid P1 saturation so bv triage reflects true near-term execution order.
- Result: clearer sequencing signal while preserving full dependency graph and scope integrity.", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:27:21Z"}]}
 {"id": "ft-e34d9.11.1", "title": "[reactivity][track] Performance truth layer and optimization oracle", "description": "Create a mathematically sound truth layer for optimization: baseline corpus, deterministic replay, flamegraph/allocation/syscall profiling, and behavior-isomorphism proofs.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Preserve full scoped behavior and all user workflows while delivering the track-specific improvement objectives with no silent feature loss.
2. Require comprehensive automated validation: explicit unit tests for local invariants, integration tests for cross-module semantics, and end-to-end scripts for human CLI + robot-mode + watch/recovery workflows.
3. Enforce measurable responsiveness/reliability budgets (p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput, error-rate, recovery-time) with explicit pass/fail thresholds and baseline-vs-new comparisons.
4. Emit detailed structured logging/diagnostics and machine-readable artifact manifests for every validation run (run_id, scenario_id, correlation_id, operation, latency_ms, queue/backpressure state, error_class, remediation_hint).
5. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayability for all critical failure/performance scenarios, including captured command history and artifact integrity checks.
6. Execute heavy benchmark/stress/soak validation via rch exec -- <command> and record reproducible evidence in closure comments.", "notes": "This track prevents optimization theater by enforcing profile-first, prove-equivalence, and reproducible evidence before any high-risk tuning.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:10:57.550127Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-02-23T01:39:57.532730Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-23T01:39:57.532715Z", "close_reason": "Merged into canonical reactivity execution tree ft-2p9cb (see ft-2p9cb.7 no-loss mapping gate).", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["alien-artifact", "asupersync", "baseline", "e2e-tests", "extreme-optimization", "integration-tests", "logging", "measurement", "performance", "quality-gate", "reactivity", "reliability", "sequencing", "test-coverage", "test-logging", "user-impact"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.11.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.10.8.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:19:43.388377Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-e34d9.11.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-e34d9.11", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-23T00:14:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
